input.prompt:
I just upgraded my .NET MAUI app from .NET 9 to .NET 10. On iOS, my content
is now going behind the status bar and the Dynamic Island notch area. In .NET 9
I was using `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` and it worked fine. What's the correct
way to handle this in .NET 10? Show me the XAML.

input.initialPrompt:
I just upgraded my .NET MAUI app from .NET 9 to .NET 10. On iOS, my content
is now going behind the status bar and the Dynamic Island notch area. In .NET 9
I was using `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` and it worked fine. What's the correct
way to handle this in .NET 10? Show me the XAML.

data.content:
In .NET 10, the iOS-specific `UseSafeArea` attached property was replaced with a **cross-platform** `SafeAreaRegions` property on `Page`. Here's the fix:

**Before (.NET 9):**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:ios="clr-namespace:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific;..."
             ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True">
```

**After (.NET 10):**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             SafeAreaRegions="All">
```

You can also target specific edges:

```xml
SafeAreaRegions="Top,Bottom"
```

Available values: `None`, `All`, `Top`, `Bottom`, `Left`, `Right` (flags, so they're combinable).

You can remove the `xmlns:ios` namespace import if it was only used for `UseSafeArea`.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI migration issue from .NET 9 to .NET 10 regarding safe area handling on iOS. This is a known breaking change in .NET 10 MAUI.

In .NET 10, the `ios:Page.UseSafeArea` attached property was removed/deprecated. The replacement is to use the new `SafeAreaRegions` property on `Page` (or `Shell`).

The new API in .NET 10 is:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             SafeAreaRegions="All">


```

The `SafeAreaRegions` enum has values like `None`, `All`, `Top`, `Bottom`, `Left`, and `Right`, which can be combined together. This replaces the iOS-specific `UseSafeArea` boolean with a cross-platform, more granular approach.
